name: Sierra de la Ubría Loop from Fuenlabrada de los Montes
name_original: Sierra de la Ubría — circular desde Fuenlabrada de los Montes
description: Hike the moderate 10.0-mile (16.1 km) Sierra de la Ubría circular route from Fuenlabrada de los Montes, offering scenic Mediterranean landsc
region_intro: Hiking trails around Fuenlabrada De Los Montes are situated in the "La Siberia Extremeña" region of Badajoz, Spain, characterized by a blend of meadows and rugged, hilly terrain. The area is part of the Cíjara Regional Reserve, featuring well-preserved Mediterranean flora including cork oaks, pines, and strawberry trees. Small streams flow through the landscape, eventually reaching the Guadalemar River. This diverse environment provides varied experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Fuenlabrada De Los Montes The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the Sierra del Chamorro and offers views of the castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Agudo to Tamurejo — circular from Baterno, a difficult 13.1 miles (21.1 km) path. It features varied terrain through the local countryside between Agudo and Tamurejo. Local hikers also love the Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop, a 14.9 miles (24.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Ubría, often completed in about 6 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Fuenlabrada De Los Montes is defined by Mediterranean forests, hilly terrain, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Fuenlabrada De Los Montes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 16 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuenlabrada De Los Montes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: Fuenlabrada de los Montes offers a diverse range of hiking trails, characterized by a blend of meadows and rugged, hilly terrain. The area is part of the Cíjara Regional Reserve, featuring well-preserved Mediterranean flora like cork oaks, pines, and strawberry trees. You'll find paths winding through these forests and along small streams that flow into the Guadalemar River.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Fuenlabrada de los Montes area?
A: There are 9 hiking routes available around Fuenlabrada de los Montes, catering to various skill levels. These include 2 easy, 5 moderate, and 2 difficult trails, ensuring options for most hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268724314/from-agudo-to-tamurejo-circular-from-baterno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Agudo to Tamurejo — circular from Baterno&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 13.1-mile (21.1 km) path featuring varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266895570/cijara-reservoir-loop-from-helechosa-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Reservoir Loop from Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.7-mile (14 km) trail.

Q: What are some points of interest or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, known for its Mediterranean forests and diverse wildlife. The region also features the Piscina Natural “Pretura de Molino” on the Guadalemar river, a natural swimming pool with picnic areas. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center&lt;/a&gt; or the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6031925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agudo&lt;/a&gt; near some routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: While specific 'easy' routes are available, the region's terrain can be rugged. For a moderate option that explores local landscapes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101999807/castillo-de-herrera-del-duque-from-herrera-del-duque-round-trip-via-sierra-del-chamorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Herrera del Duque from Herrera del Duque – round trip via Sierra del Chamorro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and takes about 2 hours 9 minutes. Always check the difficulty and length to ensure it suits your family's abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: The Cíjara Regional Reserve, where Fuenlabrada de los Montes is located, is known for its well-preserved Mediterranean flora. This suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: The routes in Fuenlabrada de los Montes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 3 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Mediterranean forests, and the opportunities to immerse themselves in the scenic landscapes of the Cíjara Regional Reserve.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Fuenlabrada de los Montes?
A: The region is part of the Cíjara Regional Reserve, which is a natural area. While many natural parks in Spain allow dogs on trails, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific regulations for dogs within the reserve should be checked locally.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266866436/herrera-del-duque-castle-and-sierra-de-la-ubria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Castle and Sierra de la Ubría Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option. This trail is 14.9 miles (24.0 km) long and typically takes around 6 hours 53 minutes to complete, leading through the Sierra de la Ubría. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268724314/from-agudo-to-tamurejo-circular-from-baterno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Agudo to Tamurejo — circular from Baterno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain around Fuenlabrada de los Montes is characterized by rugged and hilly landscapes, interspersed with meadows. You'll find paths through Mediterranean forests, featuring cork oaks and pines, and along small streams. The trails offer varied and engaging experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't provided, Fuenlabrada de los Montes is a municipality within a natural reserve. It's common for designated hiking trails in such areas to have parking facilities at or near their starting points. It's advisable to check local signage or specific route details for the most accurate parking information.

Q: Can I access the hiking trails using public transport?
A: Fuenlabrada de los Montes is situated in a rural part of Badajoz, Spain. Public transport options directly to specific trailheads might be limited. It's generally recommended to have private transportation to access the more remote hiking routes within the Cíjara Regional Reserve. Local bus services might connect to the town itself, but onward travel to trail starts may require alternative arrangements.
tips: Close to Fuenlabrada de los Montes, in the southwest, we find a mountain range that is divided into a pair, the southernmost one, called the Sierra de la Ubría. To the north is the Sierra del Chamorro.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 16.1 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 hours and 28 minutes to complete at a moderate pace.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as a moderate hike. It features an elevation gain and loss of about 290 meters, indicating a moderately undulating path. A good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with small children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and length, this trail is best suited for hikers with some experience and a good level of fitness. It might be challenging for beginners or families with very young children.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The paths are generally easily accessible, but the route is moderately undulating. You can expect to traverse Mediterranean forests and enjoy views of rolling hills typical of the Extremadura region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this route is the natural beauty of the Sierra de la Ubría itself, offering scenic views of the surrounding mountainous landscape and Mediterranean forests.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Camino del Levante - Etapa 2, Camino del Levante - Etapa 3, Camino de los Molonillos, and Camino de Herrera del Duque.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on hiking trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near agricultural land.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can experience very hot summers. The most pleasant times to hike this trail are typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and hike this trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this circular hike?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Fuenlabrada de los Montes, you can typically find parking within the town itself, near the trailhead or other public parking areas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this duration, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather conditions.
